rationale: The proof is logically complete and correct: it properly lifts subspace-open sets to X, adds the open complement to form a full cover of X, applies compactness, and intersects back to extract a finite subcover of F — no logical gaps or errors. It falls short of 5 because it omits an explicit assumption ledger (e.g., no axiom/space requirements stated) and does not surface edge cases or alternative framings a fully expert-grade treatment would include.
rubric_evidence: "let \{U_i\}_{i\in I} be any open cover of F, where each U_i is open in the subspace F"
"\{V_i\}_{i\in I}\cup\{X\setminus F\} is an open cover of X"
"By compactness of X, there exist finitely many indices i_1,\dots,i_n"
"Intersecting both sides with F gives F=\cdots=U_{i_1}\cup\cdots\cup U_{i_n}"
